## Detected Features

### API Endpoints (Backend Services)

| Method | Path | Description |
|--------|------|-------------|
| POST | `/api/users` | Create user |
| GET | `/api/users/:id` | Get user by ID |
| PUT | `/api/users/:id` | Update user |

### Web Routes (Frontend)

| Route | Component | Description |
|-------|-----------|-------------|
| `/` | HomePage | Landing page |
| `/profile` | ProfilePage | User profile |
| `/jobs` | JobsPage | Job listings |

### Data Models (Services with Database)

| Model | Table | Fields |
|-------|-------|--------|
| User | users | id, name, email, ... |
| Job | jobs | id, title, description, ... |

## Special Notes

### harborSharedModels Special Rules

```
⚠️ SPECIAL WORKFLOW APPLIES

This is the harborSharedModels repository.
Refer to: workflows/harbor-shared-models-workflow.md

Key differences:
- No pull requests
- Direct commits to main branch
- Mandatory version bump
- All services must update after changes
```

### Frontend Specific Notes

```
- Uses Next.js App Router
- Ant Design for UI components
- Redux for state management
- API client configured to use API Gateway
```

### Backend Specific Notes

```
- Express.js REST API
- JWT authentication (via API Gateway)
- PostgreSQL database with Sequelize
- Port: {specific port}
- Base path: {specific path}
```

## Actions for Agent

### When Implementing Features in This Repository

1. **Navigate to directory:**
   ```bash
   cd {repository-path}
   ```

2. **Load context:**
   - Read architecture docs (if available)
   - Understand existing patterns
   - Review code style

3. **Follow conventions:**
   - Match existing code style
   - Use same libraries
   - Follow structure patterns

4. **Test:**
   - Run `{npm run test}`
   - Run `{npm run build}`
   - Verify no TypeScript errors

---

## Memory Updates

### When to Update This Analysis

- New API endpoints added
- Structure significantly changed
- New framework/technology adopted
- Purpose clarified
- Dependencies changed

### Trigger Re-scan

```bash
# Manual re-scan of this repository
scan-repo --name {repo-name} --full
```
